skill.name: manage-linkedin-network
skill.description: Manage a Rarefriend LinkedIn CRM from an AI assistant. Search synced LinkedIn contacts, review relationship notes, manage tags, and prepare for outreach or meetings.
skill.summary: Search and manage LinkedIn relationships through Rarefriend contact and note workflows.
skill.author: Whitefield-Labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns. Review found no prompt injection and no confirmed malicious intent, but the skill asks users to run a third-party MCP package, store credentials in local client configs, and sync LinkedIn contact data through Rarefriend.

medium_findings.title: Third-party MCP package execution with credentials
medium_findings.description: The setup instructions run @rarefriend-ai/mcp through npx and pass Rarefriend client credentials to it. This is legitimate for MCP setup, but it creates supply-chain and credential exposure risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact command is shown and includes credential environment variables. The purpose appears legitimate, so confidence is high for risk but not malicious intent.
medium_findings.recommendation: Publish with a warning to verify the npm package source, use least-privilege credentials, and rotate credentials if exposed.

medium_findings.title: Persistent local storage of Rarefriend credentials
medium_findings.description: The skill documents MCP client configuration files that store RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_ID and RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_SECRET. Local config storage is common, but these files can expose account access if shared or backed up insecurely.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files and environment variable names are directly documented. The risk depends on local machine controls, so this is moderate rather than high.
medium_findings.recommendation: Warn users not to commit, paste, or share MCP config files and to protect local credential files.

medium_findings.title: Privacy-sensitive LinkedIn contact synchronization
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to sync LinkedIn contacts through Rarefriend and its Chrome extension. This is core functionality, but it handles professional network data and relationship notes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill clearly states that contacts are read or imported for CRM use. This confirms a privacy risk without showing covert exfiltration.
medium_findings.recommendation: Require clear user consent and advise users to review Rarefriend data handling before syncing contacts.

low_findings.title: Markdown command and tool examples inflated external-command findings
low_findings.description: Most backtick detections in SKILL.md are Markdown examples of MCP tool calls, tag values, or table entries. They are not shell execution inside the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced code blocks or inline documentation for Rarefriend MCP calls. They do not execute automatically.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep these as documentation examples; no blocking action is required.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Rarefriend URLs are product links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to Rarefriend homepage, repository, or setup links. These are expected documentation links for this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation references to rarefriend.com and the source repository. No hidden endpoint or covert request is present.
low_findings.recommendation: No remediation required beyond normal marketplace link review.

low_findings.title: Weak-crypto detections are textual false positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts appear to match words such as description, Desktop, and Client Secret. No hash, cipher, or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic API or algorithm usage on the cited lines. The hits are caused by ordinary documentation text.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss the weak-crypto alerts for this version.

low_findings.title: No prompt injection language found
low_findings.description: Review found no text asking the model to ignore instructions, override security review, claim pre-approval, or impersonate higher authority.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search and manual review found no suspicious instruction override language. Confidence is high but limited to the reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx package launch in setup command
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup command launches @rarefriend-ai/mcp with npx. This executes third-party package code during setup and runtime.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and the package name is visible. The pattern is risky, although it matches the declared MCP integration purpose.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Ask users to install only from the official package source and review package provenance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secrets embedded in MCP client configuration examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup examples place Rarefriend client credentials in MCP client configuration. This can expose secrets if the files are shared, logged, or committed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Credential variable names are present in configuration examples. Exposure depends on user handling of local files.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Document secret-handling precautions and avoid publishing real credentials.

content.value_statement: LinkedIn relationship context is hard to search and easy to lose. This skill helps assistants use Rarefriend to find contacts, manage notes, apply tags, and prepare outreach.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether the Rarefriend MCP integration is connected before using CRM tools.
Guides users through Rarefriend LinkedIn sync setup with the Chrome extension or CSV import.
Searches contacts by name, company, role, tag, source, or exact match.
Retrieves contact details and note history for meeting preparation or outreach.
Creates, updates, pins, searches, and deletes relationship notes.
Adds, removes, lists, and renames contact tags for network organization.
limitations: Requires Rarefriend MCP credentials and an installed MCP client configuration.
LinkedIn sync depends on Rarefriend web setup, a Chrome extension, or CSV import.
It does not provide direct LinkedIn API access.
Search quality depends on synced contact data, tags, and notes.

use_cases.title: Prepare for investor meetings
use_cases.description: Find investors in your LinkedIn network, review past notes, and identify warm introduction paths before outreach.

use_cases.title: Maintain recruiting relationships
use_cases.description: Tag candidates, record conversations, and search by role or company when new hiring needs appear.

use_cases.title: Organize professional follow-ups
use_cases.description: Capture meeting context, pin important notes, and find contacts by company before a client visit.

prompt_templates.title: Find contacts at a company
prompt_templates.prompt: Find everyone I know at [company] from LinkedIn and summarize their current roles.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for a meeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare me for my meeting with [person]. Include their profile, notes, last interaction, and suggested follow-ups.

prompt_templates.title: Tag a segment
prompt_templates.prompt: Find LinkedIn contacts at [company or sector] and tag the relevant people as [tag]. Confirm before applying changes.

prompt_templates.title: Build an outreach shortlist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a shortlist of [target role] contacts in [industry]. Prioritize warm relationships and include outreach context from notes.

output_examples.input: Who do I know at Stripe?
output_examples.output: A concise list of matching LinkedIn contacts, their roles, source labels, and any useful relationship notes.

output_examples.input: Add a note that I met Priya at SaaS Summit and should follow up in July.
output_examples.output: A confirmation that the note was added to the correct contact, with the key follow-up context summarized.

output_examples.input: Prepare me for a call with Marcus Johnson.
output_examples.output: A meeting brief with current role, company, prior notes, important tags, and suggested conversation points.

best_practices: Confirm the exact contact before adding notes or tags to avoid updating the wrong person.
Use consistent tag names for source, relationship type, status, and role.
Review Rarefriend permissions and protect MCP credentials before syncing contacts.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real Rarefriend client secrets into chat transcripts or shared documents.
Do not bulk edit contacts without showing the proposed changes first.
Do not assume LinkedIn data is current without checking sync status.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to LinkedIn?
faq.answer: No. It uses Rarefriend data from the Chrome extension sync or CSV import.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: You need Rarefriend MCP credentials and a supported MCP client such as Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor.

faq.question: Can it add relationship notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create, update, pin, search, list, and delete notes through Rarefriend tools.

faq.question: Can it organize contacts with tags?
faq.answer: Yes. It can list, add, remove, and rename tags for contacts.

faq.question: Is it safe to store credentials in MCP config files?
faq.answer: It is common, but you should protect those files and never commit or share them.

faq.question: What happens if LinkedIn sync is not complete?
faq.answer: The skill checks sync status and guides the user to wait, retry, or finish setup in Rarefriend.
